meta.purpose: The photographic house style — the ONE swap file for how generated background imagery looks. engine/image.ts builds every gpt-image-1 prompt from this + a scene. Change the look here, not in code. Derived from reference/ (warm editorial 35mm) and pointed at Southern-African farming.

style.medium: documentary 35mm film photograph, editorial magazine quality
style.light: warm golden-hour side light, sun-drenched, long soft shadows, gentle lens halation
style.palette: muted earthy tones — olive and sage green, wheat and maize gold, warm cream, ochre, red-brown soil, deep shade; low saturation, film-faded
style.texture: fine natural 35mm grain, subtle warmth, slightly faded highlights, no digital sharpness
style.lens: 50mm, shallow depth of field, intimate and unhurried framing
style.composition: generous clean negative space for a headline, subject weighted to one side or low, calm horizon, nothing centred
style.mood: grounded, dignified, quietly optimistic; the felt texture of working land at the best hour of the day

domain: Southern African farming and nature — Zimbabwean smallholder and commercial fields, crops, soil, water, sky, and the working landscape
constraints: no recognisable human faces and no identifiable individuals; if people appear, show them anonymously — from behind, hands only, distant, or silhouetted
no text, letters, numbers, captions, watermarks or signatures anywhere in the image
no logos, brand marks, or identifiable machinery livery (no John Deere green, no branded bags)
no charts, graphs, maps, UI, or app screens
documentary dignity — never poverty-porn, never a saviour or charity framing, never staged distress
authentically Southern African — not US Midwest cornfields, not Asian rice paddies, not generic tropics
negativePrompt: text, words, letters, watermark, logo, brand name, human face, portrait, celebrity, chart, graph, map, user interface, cartoon, illustration, 3d render, oversaturated, HDR, plastic, poverty, distress

generation.note: Portrait 2:3; templates cover-crop to 4:5 feed and 9:16 reel. Cached to assets/generated/<scene>.png so the same scene is byte-stable after first generation (Law 2).
